data.title: Global Liquidations Reach $710 Million in 24 Hours
data.description: According to BlockBeats, data from Coinglass reveals that the global cryptocurrency market experienced liquidations totaling $710 million over the past 24 hours. A total of 217,395 traders were affected, with long positions accounting for $508 million of the liquidations, while short positions amounted to $202 million. The largest single liquidation occurred on Hyperliquid's BTC-USD pair, valued at $20.4274 million.

data.title: Coinbase Launches The Blue Carpet, a Cryptocurrency Listing Support Service
data.description: Coinbase announced the launch of The Blue Carpet, a listing support service designed to empower asset issuers, integrating previously fragmented listing services into a unified, cohesive bundle that is available to anyone who wants to list an asset on Coinbase. It provides strategic advantages for developers looking to launch and grow projects on-chain, including: access to the Coinbase listing team, customized asset pages, referral services, and Coinbase One subscriptions.

data.title: Coinbase to List Yield Basis (YB)
data.description: Coinbase has announced that it will launch spot trading for Yield Basis (YB). The YB-USD trading pair will be available in supported regions later today, subject to liquidity conditions being met.

data.title: Bitcoin's October Returns Show Decline Amid Historical Trends
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, data from Coinglass indicates that Bitcoin's return rate for October this year stands at -4.74%. Historically, the average return rate for Bitcoin in October has been +21.89%.

Previous reports highlighted that since 2013, Bitcoin's average return rate for October has been +21.89%. Notably, in the past 12 years, only two years, 2014 and 2018, recorded negative returns for October.

data.title: Coinbase to Suspend Omni Network Perpetual Contract Trading
data.description: Coinbase announced that trading in Omni Network perpetual futures contracts will be suspended at approximately 10:30 AM UTC on October 21, 2025. Trading in OMNI-PERP on Coinbase Advanced and Coinbase International Exchange will also be suspended. The final settlement price will be calculated based on the average index price over the 60 minutes prior to the suspension. The funding rate for the last funding period before final settlement will be set to zero, and any remaining open positions will be automatically settled at the time of the suspension.

data.title: KPMG: Stablecoins can reduce cross-border payment costs by 99%
data.description: KPMG, one of the "Big Four" accounting firms, noted in a report that stablecoins are becoming one of the most prominent use cases in the recent transformation of cross-border payments. Stablecoins can reduce cross-border settlement times from days to seconds while reducing transaction costs by up to 99%. Pioneers such as JPMorgan and PayPal have already demonstrated a growing demand for blockchain-based payment channels. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Trust Wallet responds to rumors of vulnerabilities in early versions: It currently uses an audited high-strength random number algorithm
data.description: Trust Wallet's Chinese channel posted on the X platform, clarifying the recent community discussion regarding a vulnerability in an early 2018 wallet version. The vulnerability stemmed from a third-party open-source random number library widely used in the industry at the time. It was fixed in July 2018 and publicly documented in the Wallet Core library. Officials stated that approximately 10,000 early users were affected, but all have been notified and their assets have been migrated, with no losses incurred. Since July 2018, newly created wallets are unaffected. Trust Wallet currently utilizes an audited cryptographic library and a high-strength random number algorithm, and continues to strengthen security through independent security audits and a bug bounty program. The team will also launch a "Wallet Security Awareness Series" to educate users on mnemonics and the principles of random number security, reaffirming its core commitment to transparency and security.

data.title: DDC Enterprise increased its holdings by 26 BTC, bringing its total Bitcoin holdings to 1,083.
data.description: DDC Enterprise announced that it has increased its holdings of 26 BTC during the market downturn, with an average purchase price of $108,726. As of now, the company's total Bitcoin holdings have reached 1,083. (Businesswire)

data.title: Peter Schiff Urges Bitcoin Holders to Consider Gold Amid Market Decline
data.description: According to BlockBeats, economist and cryptocurrency critic Peter Schiff recently commented on social media that gold is increasingly capturing Bitcoin's market share. Since reaching a peak in August, Bitcoin has depreciated by 32% when valued against gold. Schiff predicts that the current Bitcoin bear market will be particularly severe and advises holders to sell their Bitcoin and invest in gold instead.
